changelog: Memory performance analytics + guardrail refinement. (1) New `node .claude/core/memory-manager.js analytics` report: per-category cap utilization, confidence/decay distribution, usage distribution (never-used + top-5), prune list (stale AND never-used + projected store size), injection economics, and an injection hit-rate metric (explicitly labeled a LOWER bound — only explicit recall/update are counted). Complements `lint` (hygiene) and `report` (inventory); pointer added to /review:memory-health. (2) Injection/access telemetry: session-start-prime.cjs logs `memory_injection` events; memory-manager.js searchMemories() logs `memory_access` on both SQLite and JSON paths; updateMemory() stamps `last_accessed`. All best-effort, to docs/.output/telemetry/memory-injection.jsonl. (3) Per-category memory cap hoisted to one configurable constant (MEMORY_FILTERS.MEMORY_MAX_PER_CATEGORY) with a byte-identical MEMORY_MAX_PER_CATEGORY env-override expression at both call sites (memory-manager.js + memory-guard.cjs) so they can never diverge. (4) Guardrail rules narrowed instead of all-or-nothing: rm -rf/-fr confirms EXCEPT on tmp targets (/tmp, $T*/$TMPDIR); find -exec confirms only on destructive execs (rm/mv/dd/truncate/tee/chmod/chown/sh/bash). 6 regression tests against the live rule file. Gate: 1376 passing.
